title: EU sanctions Russian GRU military hackers over cyberattacks
summary: The European Union and the United Kingdom jointly sanctioned dozens of Russian individuals and entities and accused Russia of coordinating a network of hacking groups responsible for attacks across Europe. [...]

title: US and allies warn of Russian critical infrastructure attacks
summary: Cybersecurity agencies from the United States and eight other countries have issued a joint warning that Russian state hackers are targeting vulnerable and poorly configured routers to infiltrate critical infrastructure networks. [...]

title: OpenAI temporarily relaxes GPT-5.6 Sol usage limits
summary: OpenAI is temporarily relaxing GPT-5.6 Sol usage after demand for the company's most powerful model surged over the past 48 hours. [...]

title: Claude Fable 5 stays free for paid users until July 19 as Anthropic buys more time
summary: Anthropic has just extended access to Claude Fable 5 for paid subscribers until July 19, giving you another week to keep using the most powerful model. [...]

title: RedHook Android malware now uses Wireless ADB for shell access
summary: A new version of the RedHook Android malware abuses the Android Wireless Debugging (Wireless ADB) mechanism in a novel way to gain shell-level privileges without requiring a computer connection. [...]

title: Australia warns of global campaign targeting vulnerable CMS platforms
summary: The Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC) issued an alert about a global exploitation campaign targeting vulnerable content management systems (CMS) and plugins. [...]

title: 'Ghostcommit' hides prompt injection in images to fool AI agents, steal secrets
summary: A PNG hiding a prompt injection could steal your repo's secrets, researchers demonstrate. The technique, dubbed 'Ghostcommit,' slipped past AI code reviewers CodeRabbit and Bugbot, which never open image files at all, then convinced a coding agent to read a repo's .env and write every secret into the code as a list of numbers. [...]

title: New U-Boot flaws could enable stealthy firmware attacks
summary: Six vulnerabilities in the widely used U-Boot bootloader have been discovered that could allow attackers to execute malicious code during device boot, potentially enabling stealthy firmware attacks that compromise security protections and install persistent malware. [...]

title: Ryuk ransomware member pleads guilty in the US, faces 15 years in prison
summary: A 34-year-old Armenian man has pleaded guilty to hacking U.S. companies and deploying the infamous Ryuk ransomware to encrypt their systems. [...]

title: Police suspects Dutch hackers were involved in Odido breach
summary: The Dutch National Police (Politie) says it has found "strong indications" that Dutch hackers have been involved in a February breach at the telecommunications provider Odido. [...]

title: Progress urges ShareFile admins to shut down servers over “credible” threat
summary: Progress Software is emailing ShareFile customers who use Storage Zone Controllers to immediately shut down their servers after identifying what it describes as a "credible external security threat" targeting the on-premises secure file-sharing software. [...]

title: Hackers exploit critical auth bypass in Gitea Docker image
summary: Hackers are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in the official Docker image for the Gitea self-hosted Git service that allows attackers to impersonate any user, including administrators. [...]

title: Money launderer accused of stealing seized crypto while in prison
summary: A Bulgarian national has been charged with stealing $290,000 in government-seized cryptocurrency while serving 121 months in prison for helping launder millions stolen from American fraud victims. [...]

title: The Replicant in Your Directory: AI Agents and the Identity Security Gap
summary: AI agents are accelerating the growth of non-human identities, making it harder for organizations to understand what exists, who owns it, and what it can access. Netwrix explains why stronger visibility and identity governance are essential as AI expands the enterprise attack surface. [...]

title: Zimbra urges customers to patch critical web client XSS flaw
summary: The Zimbra security team urged customers to patch a critical vulnerability affecting the Classic Web Client used to access the Zimbra Collaboration suite. [...]
